title: Phaeocephalum miliaceum
intro: Phaeocephalum miliaceum, also known as Yellow Mallow, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Phaeocephalum miliaceum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phaeocephalum miliaceum is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used as a tea or tincture to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Phaeocephalum miliaceum is classified as a species of lichen in the family Letrangeriaceae. It belongs to the genus Phaeocephalum. The classification of this species is further divided into the kingdom Fungi, phylum Ascomycota, and class Lecanoromycetes. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Phaeocephalum miliaceum. The classification also provides valuable information for further research and identification of the species.
